records.ety: [F. flageolet, dim. of OF. flaj<?/l (as if fr. a LL. flautio;us), of flaüte, flahute, F. fl<?/te. See Flute.]

defs: A small wooden pipe, having six or more holes, and a mouthpiece inserted at one end. It produces a shrill sound, softer than of the piccolo flute, and is said to have superseded the old recorder.
